metadata.title: Triton Safety Instrumented System Attack Scenario

Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Complex The Triton Safety Instrumented System Attack aims to disrupt the operation of water and wastewater systems by exploiting vulnerabilities in safety instrumented systems (SIS).
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Target Artifact (Tasks), Target Artifact (Application), Script File, User Account, Target Artifact (Android), Network Traffic, File System, Target Artifact (Alternate)
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Artificial Intelligence, Reg.exe Utility, Custom Tool Development, Code Injection, Scheduled Task/Job, Web Shell, OS API Execution (Mechanism), User Account Authentication (Mechanism), System Information Discovery, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), File Access (Mechanism), Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol, Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker gathers information about water and wastewater systems using artificial intelligence.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker identifies potential entry points and vulnerabilities in the system by monitoring network traffic.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attacker creates custom tools to exploit identified vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2021-44168: Fortinet FortiOS "execute restore src-vis" downloads code without integrity checking, allowing an attacker to arbitrarily download files.
stage.description: The attacker tricks an employee into revealing login credentials by sending a spear phishing link.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-21839: Oracle WebLogic Server contains an unspecified vulnerability that allows an unauthenticated attacker with network access via T3, IIOP, to compromise Oracle WebLogic Server.
stage.description: The attacker exploits a vulnerability in a website visited by employees to gain initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2013-4810: HP ProCurve Manager (PCM), PCM+, Identity Driven Manager (IDM), and Application Lifecycle Management allow remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a marshalled object to (1) EJBInvokerServlet or (2) JMXInvokerServlet.
stage.description: The attacker injects malicious code into the system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2019-20085: TVT devices utilizing NVMS-1000 software contain a directory traversal vulnerability via GET /.. requests.
stage.description: The attacker schedules a task to run at a later time.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC-650: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: The attacker establishes a persistent backdoor.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker schedules a task to maintain persistence.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker elevates privileges to gain control over the system.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attacker avoids detection by antivirus software.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker attempts to guess passwords.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker gathers information about the system.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attacker uses valid accounts to move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attacker uses remote services to move laterally.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker collects sensitive data from the local system.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates data using alternative protocols.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts the operation of the water and wastewater systems.

Scenario.summary: The Triton Safety Instrumented System Attack is a complex attack that targets water and wastewater systems. It involves multiple stages, including reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.
